agenda_subtitle: Fixed operating sequence for each top-level sales region

forbidden_behaviors: Do not redesign layout during monthly population.
Do not invent metrics or horizons not present in the validated fact pack.
Do not relabel ACV as ARR or hide Omitted inside active headline pipeline.
Do not present the Q1 promise baseline as a clean commitment number without qualification.

slides.title: Regional Executive Summary
slides.subtitle: Headline position, leadership actions, and quarter focus

cards.label: All Open Pipeline
cards.body: Populate the full open-book ARR in EUR converted. Keep this separate from FY26 and Q2 so the headline does not overstate the near-term position.

cards.label: FY26 Active Pipeline
cards.body: Populate FY26 active ARR in EUR converted, excluding Omitted from the active headline and stating that rule plainly.

cards.label: Q2 Active Pipeline
cards.body: Populate current-quarter active ARR in EUR converted. Use this card to anchor whether coverage is healthy, thin, or concentrated.

cards.label: Open Renewal Book
cards.body: Populate open renewal ACV in EUR converted. Renewals stay ACV, and the slide should identify the single leadership action on the renewal book.

source_fields: Pipeline ARR — All Open (any close date)
Pipeline ARR — FY26 Close Dates Only (excl. Omitted)
Pipeline ARR — Q2 2026 Close Dates Only (excl. Omitted)
metric_rules: Pipeline headline metrics remain ARR in EUR converted.
Renewals remain ACV in EUR converted.
Top risk and top action must be synthesized from validated facts, not invented.
known_gaps: Leadership action is editorial and must be grounded in validated metrics rather than a raw workbook field.

slides.title: Q1 Promised vs Delivered
slides.subtitle: Regional actuals, slipped volume, and baseline qualification

cards.label: Won in Q1
cards.body: Show regional won count and ARR delivered in quarter. This is the delivered outcome, not a forecast statement.

cards.label: Lost in Q1
cards.body: Show regional lost count and ARR with a factual read on what was lost. Do not inflate this into a qualitative post-mortem.

cards.label: Slipped out of Q1
cards.body: Use validated slipped count and ARR aggregated across the region. This should be one of the main messages of the slide.

cards.label: Promise Baseline
cards.body: Only state the regional promise baseline if it is forecast-safe. Otherwise qualify the ambiguity explicitly and do not present it as a clean commitment number.

metric_rules: Won, lost, and slipped are validated regional rollups from director-scoped actuals and pushed-deal populations.
Promise baseline must remain explicitly qualified.
known_gaps: Promise baseline is aggregated from director-level pipeline inspection populations and is not a forecast-safe commitment number.

slides.title: Quarterly Pipeline and Forecast
slides.subtitle: Display-quarter regional book, forecast mix, and omitted separation

cards.label: Quarter Active Pipeline
cards.body: Populate active displayed-quarter ARR in EUR converted. This is the near-term regional pipeline headline.

cards.label: Commit
cards.body: Populate displayed-quarter Commit ARR and frame it as forecast mix, not the total quarter story.

cards.label: Best Case
cards.body: Populate displayed-quarter Best Case ARR and make clear how much of the quarter depends on conversion from Best Case.

cards.label: Omitted
cards.body: Keep Omitted ARR visible and clearly separated from the active headline so the region does not hide excluded exposure.

source_fields: Pipeline ARR — Q2 2026 Close Dates Only (excl. Omitted)
metric_rules: Use ARR in EUR converted for all four cards.
Keep Omitted separate from active headline pipeline.
If current-quarter in-scope regional pipeline is zero, the slide may fall forward to next-quarter outlook only with explicit footnote disclosure.

slides.title: Regional Book Breakdown
slides.subtitle: Subregions and MD-1 books inside the regional rollup

metric_rules: Regional book membership follows forecast hierarchy, not ad hoc grouping.
Middle East & Africa belongs under EMEA.
known_gaps: This slide is a deterministic rollup and mapping view, not a direct single-tab workbook extract.

body_guidance: List each component book in the rollup with the responsible MD-1.
Call out where concentration or underperformance sits inside the region.
Preserve the forecast hierarchy: ME&A belongs under EMEA, not APAC.

slides.title: Pipeline Coverage and Opportunity Intel
slides.subtitle: Coverage statement, top regional opportunities, and hygiene pressure

cards.label: Coverage Position
cards.body: State whether regional Q2 coverage is healthy, thin, or concentrated. The wording should sound like an executive readout, not a dashboard label.

cards.label: Top Opportunities
cards.body: List only the most material current opportunities with ARR, stage, and next step. Keep the list short and leadership-relevant.

cards.label: Hygiene and Intel
cards.body: Summarize stale ARR, aging ARR, data-quality backlog, and competitor signals. Use this panel to explain why the quarter may be less healthy than headline pipeline suggests.

metric_rules: Top opportunities on this slide must be quarter-filtered displayed-quarter active opportunities, not all-open long-dated deals.
Coverage statement is editorial but must be anchored to displayed-quarter active ARR and weighted pipeline.
known_gaps: Competitive loss watchlist is limited to explicit external-competitor loss reasons present in top regional loss rows.

slides.title: Commercial Approval Overview
slides.subtitle: Regional stage 3+ control view for approvals and exceptions

cards.label: Approval Rate
cards.body: Insert the validated approval rate for stage 3+ opportunities and disclose the method if the denominator is approximate.

cards.label: Approved
cards.body: Show approved deal count and ARR so leadership can distinguish approved volume from the control backlog.

cards.label: Pending or Missing
cards.body: Show the backlog still pending or missing approval. Keep it operational and action-oriented.

cards.label: Missing Candidates
cards.body: Show the count of stage 3+ land opportunities still missing approval and set up the next slide as the action list.

metric_rules: Approved and pending exposure are ARR in EUR converted.
Approval rate method must be disclosed when denominator is approximate.
known_gaps: Regional approval rate is currently a simple average of component-book approval-rate metrics, not a direct unified denominator.

slides.title: Missing Commercial Approval Candidates
slides.subtitle: Regional land stage 3+ opportunities requiring immediate attention

metric_rules: Candidates must be listed largest ARR first.
Only validated stage 3+ land opportunities missing approval belong here.

body_guidance: Include opportunity, ARR, owner, region or book, stage, and next required action.
Keep the list factual; do not infer approval status beyond the validated snapshot.

slides.title: Renewals and Retention
slides.subtitle: Current-quarter renewal book, likelihood, and watchlist

cards.label: Open Renewal Book
cards.body: Insert regional renewal deal count and ACV in EUR converted. Keep this explicitly as ACV, not ARR.

cards.label: Likelihood and Risk
cards.body: Summarize renewal risk distribution and call out sparse tagging if needed. If tagging is weak, say that plainly.

cards.label: Watchlist
cards.body: List only the renewals that need active leadership follow-up this quarter, with owner and next checkpoint.

metric_rules: Renewal values remain ACV in EUR converted.
Watchlist should prioritize current-quarter renewals.
known_gaps: Renewal risk tagging may be sparse; if so, the slide must say that plainly instead of overstating segmentation quality.

slides.title: Slipped Deals and Root Cause
slides.subtitle: Lead with validated slipped deals; expand commentary with book-level owner input

metric_rules: Lead with slipped count and slipped ARR.
Root-cause summary must distinguish validated reasons from owner follow-up needed.
known_gaps: Owner-level root cause commentary may be incomplete and should be marked as follow-up instead of fabricated.

body_guidance: Lead with slipped count and slipped ARR.
List the largest slipped opportunities and the stated reason if available.
If root-cause commentary is incomplete, mark the gap and route to book-owner follow-up.

slides.title: Churn Risk and Finance Inputs
slides.subtitle: Hold as an explicit placeholder until Finance inputs are operationalized

metric_rules: No churn metrics may be inferred from workbook data.
known_gaps: Finance-owned churn trend inputs are not operationalized in the current workbook contract.

body_guidance: State what Finance input is currently available and what is missing.
Name the owner or source for the churn reporting path when known.
Do not fabricate churn trend numbers.

slides.title: Appendix and Factual Notes
slides.subtitle: Definitions, source lineage, and known limitations

metric_rules: Appendix notes must restate ARR vs ACV and omitted-stage rules.
Known limitations should be explicit rather than buried.

body_guidance: Note that Omitted remains excluded from active headline pipeline.
Document forecast-hierarchy rules and any data-quality limitations.
